pairs.instruction: What key do I press to move forward in Cubyz?
What key moves the player backward?
Which key strafes the player left?
Which key strafes the player right?
What key makes the player sprint?
What key makes the player jump?
How do you crouch in Cubyz?
What key toggles flying in Cubyz?

How do you descend while flying in Cubyz?
pairs.response: Left shift -- the same key used for crouching and falling while flying.

What keys select hotbar slots 1 through 9 in Cubyz?
pairs.response: The number keys 1 through 9, in order.

pairs.instruction: What keys select hotbar slots 10, 11, and 12 in Cubyz?
pairs.response: 0 selects slot 10, - selects slot 11, and = selects slot 12.

pairs.instruction: Who created Cubyz and when?
pairs.response: zenith391 and ZaUserA created it on August 22, 2018.

pairs.instruction: Who currently maintains Cubyz?
Who are the original creators of Cubyz, as opposed to who maintains it today?
pairs.response: zenith391 and ZaUserA created it in 2018; today it's maintained by IntegratedQuantum (QuantumDeveloper).

pairs.instruction: What was Cubyz originally called before its current name?
Before it was called Cubyz, what was this game's name?
When was the project renamed from Cubz to Cubyz?
pairs.response: March 17, 2019, when zenith391 suggested the new name in Discord.

pairs.instruction: When did the Zig rewrite of Cubyz begin?
Why was Cubyz rewritten from Java to Zig?
pairs.response: Java's garbage collector caused freeze-inducing lag spikes when the memory limit was hit; Zig's built-in cross-compilation support (letting QuantumDeveloper build for Windows and test via Wine) was also a major draw over alternatives like C++20 or Rust.

pairs.instruction: When was Cubyz version 0.0.0 released?
How much health does the player start with in Cubyz?
pairs.response: 8 hearts, for a total of 16 health points.

pairs.instruction: Can the player heal in Cubyz?
pairs.response: No -- there is currently no way to regain health other than dying.

pairs.instruction: Is there any way to regain lost health in Cubyz?
pairs.response: No -- currently the only way to reset your health is to die.

pairs.instruction: What does the energy/stomach bar do in Cubyz?
pairs.response: Currently nothing -- it's purely cosmetic. There's no way to gain or lose energy yet.

pairs.instruction: How many movement modes does the player have in Cubyz?
How high can the player jump in Cubyz?
pairs.response: Enough elevation to cross one whole block.

pairs.instruction: How many hotbar slots does Cubyz have by default?
How many items can a single hotbar or inventory slot stack in Cubyz?
How many slots does the main inventory have in Cubyz?
What tool is used to mine stone, metal, and gem blocks in Cubyz?
What tool is used to damage or mine soil blocks in Cubyz?
What tool is used to mine wooden blocks in Cubyz?
What tool is used to harvest plants, leaves, and cloth in Cubyz?
pairs.response: Sickle -- it's also the only tool that lets you collect plants and leaves at all, which isn't possible bare-handed.

pairs.instruction: What happens if you use the wrong tool type on a block in Cubyz?
pairs.response: It deals the same damage as using your bare hands -- no bonus, no penalty.

pairs.instruction: Is Cubyz available on Mac? Why or why not?
pairs.response: No -- Mac is not supported because it lacks OpenGL 4.3.

pairs.instruction: What operating systems does Cubyz support?
How does Cubyz send player position updates over the network?
pairs.response: Via the playerPosition struct, which handles sending and receiving player position updates and ensures they're sent no more than once every 50 milliseconds.

pairs.instruction: What is the default multiplayer server port for Cubyz?
Which port does a Cubyz server listen on unless I change it?
How do I grant a player full admin permissions via the Cubyz permission command?
How do I grant a player access to just the /spawn command in Cubyz?
What network protocol does Cubyz use for its multiplayer port forwarding?
How far can the Cubyz world extend on each axis?
pairs.response: 2 billion blocks on each axis -- not literally infinite, but effectively so for gameplay purposes.

pairs.instruction: How big is the Cubyz world -- is it truly infinite?
pairs.response: Not literally infinite, but effectively so for gameplay purposes -- the world extends 2 billion blocks on each axis.

pairs.instruction: What happens if an addon references a block ID that doesn't exist?
pairs.response: The engine doesn't crash -- it logs "Couldn't find block {id}. Replacing it with air..." and silently substitutes air (block index 0) for the missing reference.

pairs.instruction: Why might a biome's custom sky or fog color not show up in-game even though it's set in the Addon Creator?
pairs.response: Likely a format mismatch, not a mistake on your end -- the Addon Creator website exports skyColor/fogColor as a 3-component float vector, but the engine's biome loader reads those fields as a single packed u32 integer, which typically fails silently and falls back to the default color.
